look_at.text: A whiteboard covered in org charts and initiative timelines. Someone has drawn a large box in the corner labelled 'QUICK WINS'. The box is empty.

use.text: I'm not writing on Bob's whiteboard.

look_at.text: A pair of noise-cancelling headphones hanging unused. In an office like this, silence is a luxury you have to engineer yourself.

pick_up.text: Bob's headphones. Not mine to take.

use.text: I'm not here to listen to anything. I'm here to leave.

look_at.text: Bob looks like a man who has accepted his fate gracefully. He's waiting for the document.
Bob is watching you. He seems marginally calmer now. The agreement is in play. He's waiting.
Bob squeezes the stress ball rhythmically. He's visibly more composed. Something approaching human.
Bob. Senior Director, Amazon HR. He has the look of a man who has processed a hundred of these and still finds each one personally expensive.

talk_to.text: Bob raises an eyebrow. 'You have the co-signed document?' He extends his hand.
Bob nods. 'You have the document. Now you just need to sign it. And... co-sign it. You know what that means.'

pick_up.text: I'm not picking up Bob. Not even in a metaphorical sense.

combinations.text: Bob looks at it. Then at you. Then back at it. He takes it. 'Where did you get this?' He squeezes it once. Some tension leaves his face. 'Fine. Sit down.'
Bob takes the stress ball. He squeezes it. Something in him unclenches slightly.
Bob stares at it. 'That's... addressed to Chen. They were let go on Tuesday.' He takes it, slowly. 'I'll deal with it.' Something resolves in his expression. 'Right. Let me pull up your file.'
You hand over the package. Bob takes it without a word. He sets it behind the desk. 'Closure,' he says, quietly. 'Right. Your turn.'

Bob takes the document. He reads it — actually reads it — line by line. He reaches the bottom. He looks up. 'This is in order. Everything checks out.' He initials the final page. 'Alex. You're free. I hope it's better out there than it was in here.'
You hand Bob the co-signed agreement. He reviews it carefully, nods, and adds his initials. 'You're done here, Alex. Good luck.'
'It needs a co-signature. You know whose.' He taps the business card column on the document. 'The credential has to be verified.'
'You haven't signed it yet. And it needs to be co-signed. You know the process.' He says this without irony.
Bob glances at it. 'What is this for?' He turns it over. 'I'm not plugging a random USB into my machine.' He hands it back.
Bob looks at the Jeff Bezos business card for a long time. 'Where did you get this.' It isn't a question. He doesn't take it.
You hand Bob 'Winning Ugly'. He reads the cover. He reads the subtitle. 'Why are you showing me this.' He hands it back. He looks like a man filing something unpleasant away for later.
You hold out the frozen egg. Bob looks at it. He looks at you. 'I have seventeen open cases.' He returns to his screen. The egg is still yours.
You produce the damp egg. Bob stares at it. 'I don't know what that is. I don't want to know what that is.' He points at the door.
You offer the kitchen coffee. He sniffs it. 'That's from the drip machine.' He gestures to his own untouched mug. 'I have one. Also from the drip machine. I know.'

look_at.text: A small foam stress ball on the desk. It's been squeezed so many times the Amazon logo is barely legible and there's a permanent fist-shaped dent in the side. Someone has been working through some things.

pick_up.text: That's on Bob's desk. I'm not taking it while he's sitting right there.

use.text: Maybe I should give it to Bob. He seems like he could use it.

look_at.text: The computer shows the agreement file as opened. The printer tray has a copy in it.
The screen is unlocked now. Bob's HR system is open. Your file is right there, but the agreement is a locked PDF. I need the USB stick to open it.
Bob's computer is logged in. His HR case management system shows your file. The separation agreement is there — but it's locked behind an access token. There's a USB port on the front.
Bob's computer. The screen is on but faced away slightly. I can see it's a case management system. Can't interact while he's watching me like that.

use.text: The document is already printed and retrieved. Nothing more to do here.
Bob's system is open. I can see my file. The agreement PDF is right there but password-locked. The USB stick might have what I need.
Bob nods permission. But the agreement file is locked. 'Token-based access. You'd need an authorised USB device to open it.' He doesn't explain further.
Bob's not going to let me touch his computer right now.

combinations.text: You plug in the USB stick. The system recognises it — 'CVNA BUILD v4.1 — Token Accepted'. The separation agreement unlocks. Bob prints it without a word. It slides from the printer tray, still warm.
You slot in the USB stick. The access system activates. But the agreement is still locked — something else is needed first before the file will open.
Bob doesn't look up. 'Put that away.'

look_at.text: A framed Leadership Principle printout. All fourteen, laminated, centred. Bob has highlighted two in yellow marker: 'Deliver Results' and 'Are Right, A Lot'. The irony appears unintentional.

use.text: I don't need to interact with the Leadership Principles. I just need to survive them.

look_at.text: The printer tray has a document in it — your separation agreement. Still warm.
A network printer with an unusually short queue for an Amazon office. One job pending. Probably Bob's.

use.text: You take the printed agreement from the tray.
Nothing in the tray right now.

look_at.text: A tall storage cupboard. Closed. These things always contain either nothing useful or something you shouldn't see.

open.text: You pull the door open. Inside: stacked reams of paper, some branded Amazon merch, and rolling loose at the back — a ballpoint pen.

close.text: You push the cupboard door shut.

look_at.text: A black ballpoint pen. Amazon-branded. Of course it is.

use.text: I should PICK UP the pen first.

open.text: You head back to the open office.

use.text: You head back to the open office.
